title: Cocoa Canadian Dates Stir-fry
description: A savory and light date-night dish featuring cocoa, sautéed with whole30-friendly Canadian dates in a quick and easy preparation.
instructions: Soak the Canadian Dates in hot water for about 20 minutes.
Drain and rinse the soaked dates, then chop them into small pieces.
Place chopped dates on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes until they are caramelized and slightly browned.
While dates are roasting, heat almond oil or avocado oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add chopped dates to the skillet, along with cocoa powder. Stir well until everything is evenly coated with cocoa powder.
Pour balsamic vinegar into the skillet and continue stirring for another 2-3 minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.
Remove from heat, add rosemary sprigs and a pinch of salt and pepper to taste, and mix thoroughly.
Let the dish cool down slightly before serving.
